heading: Offenses not capital
label: 3282 Offenses not capital
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Aliens and Nationality, and on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 582 (R.S. § 1044; , ; , ; , ; , title I, subchap. III, § 346(g), ).section 746(g) of title 8Apr. 13, 1876, ch. 5619 Stat. 32Nov. 17, 1921, ch. 124, § 142 Stat. 220Dec. 27, 1927, ch. 645 Stat. 51Oct. 14, 1940, ch. 87654 Stat. 1167, U.S.C., 1940 ed., and , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Aliens and Nationality, were consolidated. “Except as otherwise expressly provided by law” was inserted to avoid enumeration of exceptive provisions.Section 582 of title 18section 746(g) of title 8The proviso contained in the act of 1927 “That nothing herein contained shall apply to any offense for which an indictment has been heretofore found or an information instituted, or to any proceedings under any such indictment or information,” was omitted as no longer necessary.In the consolidation of these sections the 5-year period of limitation for violations of the Nationality Code, provided for in said , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Aliens and Nationality, is reduced to 3 years. There seemed no sound basis for considering 3 years adequate in the case of heinous felonies and gross frauds against the United States but inadequate for misuse of a passport or false statement to a naturalization examiner.section 746(g) of title 8

Amendments2003— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 108–211954—Act , changed the limitation period from three years to five years.Sept. 1, 1954

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as otherwise expressly provided by law, no person shall be prosecuted, tried, or punished for any offense, not capital, unless the indictment is found or the information is instituted within five years next after such offense shall have been committed.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In any indictment for an offense under chapter 109A for which the identity of the accused is unknown, it shall be sufficient to describe the accused as an individual whose name is unknown, but who has a particular DNA profile.

children.label: A
children.text: the limitations period described under subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: the provisions of chapter 208 until the individual is arrested or served with a summons in connection with the charges contained in the indictment.

children.heading: .—Defined term
children.label: 3 .—Defined term
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “DNA profile” means a set of DNA identification characteristics.
